sections.paragraph:
χρυσοῦς, ῆ, οῦν (χρυσός; Trag., Attic wr.; ins, pap, LXX; En 99:7; TestSol; TestAbr B 10 p. 114, 26 [Stone p. 76]; TestJob, JosAs; GrBar 6:7; ApcMos 33; EpArist, Philo; Tat 19, 1. Contracted from χρύσεος: Hom. et al.; ins; Just., D. 20, 4; rare in pap and LXX [Thackeray p. 173]. Both forms in Joseph. [Schmidt 491]. Uncontracted forms are found in our lit. only as v.l. in Rv: χρυσέων 2:1, χρυσέους 4:4, χρυσέας 5:8); the acc. fem. sing. χρυσᾶν Rv 1:13 instead of v.l. χρυσῆν is formed on the analogy of ἀργυρᾶν (s. PGM 10, 26 χρυσᾶν ἢ ἀργυρᾶν and B-D-F §45; s. Mlt-H. 120f; Psaltes p. 187f) made of/adorned with gold, golden 2 Ti 2:20; Hb 9:4ab; Rv 1:12f, 20; 2:1; 4:4; 5:8; 8:3ab; 9:13, 20; 14:14; 15:6f; 17:4; 21:15; Dg 2:7.—Golden in color or appearance Hv 4, 1, 10; 4, 3, 4 (Demetr.: 722 Fgm. 1, 15 p. 669 Jac. ‘goldpiece’).—DELG s.v. χρυσός. M-M. s.v.-εος.

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

  1. wr. = writers, writings

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

En En = I Enoch—List 2

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

ApcMos ApcMos = Apocalypse of Moses—List 2

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Thackeray Thackeray = HT., A Grammar of the OT in Greek according to the Septuagint—List 6

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

acc. acc. = accusative

fem. fem. = feminine

sing. sing. = singular

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Mlt-H. Mlt-H. = JMoulton/WHoward, A Grammar of NT Greek II: Accidence and Word-formation—List 6

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

Hv Hv = Visions

Demetr Demetr , III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1093
